modules.title: What is a Variable?
modules.content:
A variable is a named container for a value. When your code runs, that name points to a spot in memory where the value lives. You can read it, change it, or pass it around — it's the most fundamental building block in any program.

In JavaScript, you declare a variable using one of three keywords: `var`, `let`, or `const`. Each has different rules about where it can be used and whether its value can change.

modules.title: let and const
modules.content:
`let` declares a variable whose value can be reassigned later. Use it when you know the value will need to change — like a counter, or a user's current score.

`const` declares a variable that cannot be reassigned. Once you set it, that binding is locked. Use `const` by default — it makes your intent clear and prevents accidental overwrites. If you later find you need to reassign it, switch it to `let` then.

Both `let` and `const` are block-scoped, meaning they only exist within the `{}` block they were declared in.

modules.title: What about var?
modules.content:
`var` is the original way to declare variables in JavaScript, but it behaves differently from `let` and `const` in ways that can cause subtle bugs. It is function-scoped rather than block-scoped, and it gets 'hoisted' to the top of its scope — meaning you can technically reference it before the line it was declared on without an error, though its value will be `undefined`.

Modern JavaScript code avoids `var`. You will still encounter it in older codebases, so it is worth understanding, but prefer `let` and `const` in anything you write.

modules.title: Knowledge Check
modules.question: You are tracking a user's score in a game. The score will increase as they play. Which keyword should you use to declare it?
options: const, because scores are important and should be protected
let, because the value needs to be reassigned as the score changes
var, because it works anywhere in the function
int, because scores are always whole numbers

modules.title: Knowledge Check
modules.question: What will happen if you try to reassign a variable declared with `const`?
options: The new value will be assigned silently
JavaScript will use the old value and ignore the new one
An error will be thrown — const bindings cannot be reassigned
It depends on whether you are in strict mode
